The Mahdi in the Holy Quran.

Who are more unjust than those who prevent the name of Allah from being mentioned in His mosques and strive to destroy them. It is not for them to enter them except in fear. For them in this world is disgrace and they will have in the Hereafter a great punishment

Ibn Jarir Tabari says in his commentary; "As for their disgrace in this world, it will be when the Mahdi rises and Constantinople is liberated. He will kill and capture them. That will be their disgrace". Qurtubi relates from Qatada and Suddi; "Their disgrace in this world will be when the Mahdi rises and Amorium, Rome, and Constantinople are liberated"
Shawkani says; "As for their disgrace in this world, it is when the Mahdi rises and Constantinople is liberated"
Qurtubi also narrates from Suddi concerning the verse
To make it manifest over all other religions
; "that is when the Mahdi emerges. No one will remain without either accepting Islam or paying the J
And indeed he is a sign of the Hour so be not in any doubt concerning it and follow me. This is the straight path

As a commentary of the verse
When the promise of the Hereafter comes, (We will send) to
, Ibn Kathir quoted another verse as it's explanation. He quoted the verse
For them is disgrace in this world
. Then he said "They have explained the disgrace in this world by the emergence of the Mahdi- the likes of Suddi, Ikr
The following are some of the most important and authentic narrations which speak of the Mahdi:
It is reported from Abu Saeed Khudri (r.a) that the Prophet
ahdi is from me, a prominent forehead, a raised nose bridge, he will fill the earth with justice and equity as it was filled with injustice and oppression. He will rule for seven years".
It is reported from Abu Saeed Khudri (r.a) that the Prophet

said; "In the ending phase of my ummah, the Mahdi will emerge. Allah will send the rains for him. The earth will send forth its produce. He will distribute wealth equally. Livestock will be in abundance. The Ummah will become great. He will live for seven or eight" (years).
Narrated from him as well, the Prophet

said; "I give you the glad tidings of the Mahdi who will be sent at a time of great discord among men, and earthquakes. He will fill the earth with justice and equity as it was filled with injustice and oppression. The
of the skies and the earth will be pleased with him. He will distribute wealth equally". A man asked; "Equally"? He
replied "Equally among people". The narrator says; "Allah will fill the hearts of the Ummah of Muhammad
with his justice to the extent that he will command an

Then only one man will stand. They will tell him to go to the treasurer and tell them "The Mahdi says to give me wealth". They will say to him; "Pour out for him". Then when he gathers it up he will regret and say; "I am the greediest

! Was it not sufficient for me, what was sufficient for them?!" So he will go to return it but they will refuse to accept it. They will say; "We do not take back something we gave". It will be like that for seven or eight or nine years. Then
will be no good in living after that". Or he said; "there will be no good in life after that".
Ali (r.a), from the Messenger of Allah

Narrated from Umm Salama (r.a), she said; "I heard the Messenger of Allah

In the narration of Tirmidhi; "The world will not go, or end, until a man from my family household rules the Arabs. His name will correspond to mine". When he
means all the Muslims who follow him, because everyone who becomes a Muslim, is an Arab." Tibi explains it as; "He

as well' even though it is meant, because when he rules the Arabs, and they become united as one hand, they will dominate all other nations. It could also be said that the reason he only mentioned 'Arabs' was because they were dominant at his time, or because they are the noblest, or he simply
for brevity. Nevertheless, both Arabs and
are meant. Like when Allah says
garments which protect you from the heat

knows best." …knowing fully well that in other narrations it is known that he will conquer Daylam, Constantinople, Rome and India, and all these are lands of the non-Arabs.
Imam Ahmad narrates from Zir, from Abdullah (r.a) from the Prophet
he Hour will not be established until a man from my family household takes authority. His name will correspond to mine.

Imam Ahmad narrates from Ali (r.a); "If only one day remained of time, Allah will send a man from my family household who will fill it with justice as it was filled with oppression." as it was filled with oppression."

hen they reach at Baidaa (a large expanse of land) they will sink." So we said, 'Oh Messenger of Allah, the road brings together different people?' He replied, "Yes, there are those who know very well what they

Allah will resurrect them according to their intentions."
From Abu Huraira (r.a), the Messenger of Allah

qama will be given to begin the prayer and it would be said to him, 'Go forward, Oh spirit of Allah'. He will respond, 'Your leader should go forward and lead you in prayer'.

of narrations about the Mahdi. The last one he brought was the narration of Jabir found in Sahih Muslim which he wrote afterwards; "There is no mention of the Mahdi in it, however these types of narrations cannot be referring to anyone except the awaited Mahdi as the aforementioned
Harith ibn Abi Rabi'a, Abdullah ibn Safwan and

refuge at the house so an army will be sent towards him. Then when they reach Baida, they will sink.

and Allah will liberate it. Then Persia, and Allah will liberate it. Then you will fight Rome and Allah will liberate it. Then you will fight the Dajjaal and Allah will give you victory". Naaif' said, "Jabir, we do not think that the Dajjaal will emerge until Rome is liberated." The reason for bringing this
adith is because these expeditions which will

This is known from the narrations mentioned before which are found in Bukhari and Muslim which states that the one to kill the Dajjaal is Isa the son of Maryam (a.s) and he will descend when the Mahdi would have been preparing to lead the prayer as their Imam and Caliph.
Who is Imam Mahdi (a.s), the rightly guided Caliph, Muhammad ibn Abdullah?
In the end of time, a rightly guided Caliph will emerge from the family lineage of the Prophet
said, "The Mahdi is from us, the family household." Allah will strengthen the religion using him. He will reign for seven, eight or nine years. He will fill the earth with
and equity as it was fill with injustice and oppression.
The Muslim nation will enjoy bounties in his time, the likes of which they never experienced before. The earth will send forth its vegetation. The skies will send forth its rain. The Prophet
said, "My nation will enjoy bounties in the time of the Mahdi, the likes of which they
enjoyed before. The skies will rain on them abundantly and the earth will not leave anything of its vegetation without sending it forth". He will give wealth without counting. The Prophet
said, "He will distribute wealth equally". He was asked what was meant by
". In his time, the fruits will be in abundance. Crops will be
Good will be perpetual in his time. Wealth will be overflowing. And the kingship will be powerful. The Prophet

said, "The Mahdi will emerge in the ending of my nation. Allah will send upon him rains. The earth will send forth its vegetation. He will distribute wealth equally. Livestock will be plenty. And the Ummah will be great". The
een (religion) will be established and the enemy would be muffled. The Prophet
en in the end of time, as I established it in the beginning of time."

The Mahdi is from the progeny of Hasan or Husein, both the children of Ali (may Allah be pleased with them). It comes in the narrations that Ali looked at his son Hasan and said, "This son of mine is a Sayyid as the Messenger of Allah
called him. Coming from his loins will be a man

the name of your Prophet. He will fill the earth with justice as it was filled with oppression and injustice". In another hadith; "He is from this son of mine" and he placed his hand on Husein. It is also said that the Mahdi will come from both lines. The Prophet
who sent me with the Haqq! From them – Hasan and Husein – is the Mahdi of this Ummah.
The kunya of a person is the name the person carries on the
of 'Abu So and So'. The kunya of the Mahdi will be Abu Abd
said, "His kunya will be Abu Abd
llah". His kunya will also be Abu al Qasim. The Prophet
said, "His kunya will be like my kunya". The kunya of the Prophet
His name will be Muhammad while his father's name will be Abdullah. The Prophet
said, "His name will correspond to mine and his father's name will correspond to my father's". The name of his mother will be Amina.
said, "…we recognized him by his name, his father's name, his mother's name and his descriptions".

Muhammad Isa Dawood narrates, "The name of his mother is Amina (which means safeguarded in Arabic). She will be safeguarded from every evil. Her father and mother will die while she is still young and Allah will bring her up". The Mahdi is also Fatimi as he comes from the progeny of Fatima Zahraa, the daughter of the Prophet
said; "The Mahdi is from my special family lineage, from the children of Fatima".
His birthplace is either in Makkah or Madinah. Ali (r.a) said, "The Mahdi, his birthplace is Madinah".
said, "A man will emerge from the family of my household in the Haram". He will be Qureshi and Hashimi, though his lineage will go back to Yemen. In other words, his hometown will be in Yemen.
said, "The Mahdi is but from the Quraish and Caliphate is not but from them. However, his origin and lineage
will hail from a village in
He will hail from Tihama in Yemen.
said, "He will emerge from Tihama". He will come from a village called Kar'a (or Qar'a as the word comes in other narrations.
said, "The Mahdi will emerge from a village in

Yemen called Kar'a". This village lies to the extreme west of Yemen, south of the city Hudaida, east of Bait Faqih, the furthest point on the boundary of eastern Tihama and the beginning point of the border of Raima in the west. It rests as a pass between mountains; the Bani Qahwa and Jehzan
. It is a village comprising of many

smaller sub-villages; Dhiha, Mikra', Jerwa, Haajar and Marhala. It is connected to Uzla Hawadil. Tihama and Raima fights for it. It is connected to Raima in terms of Governing. But it is connected to Tihama culturally and geographically. Its population also speaks the Tihami dialect.
He will grow up in Hijaz and begin preaching at the age of eighteen. The Prophet
"The Mahdi is a man with arched brows and bright eyes. He will come from Hijaz and ascend the pulpit of Damascus when he is at the age of eighteen

" It seems that the word 'Damascus' was mistakenly inserted into to the narration. Or it could mean that he will go from Hijaz to Yemen at the age of eighteen then climb the pulpit of Damascus in preparation for the Grand Battle after the pledge was given in Makkah. As for him being deported or forced to leave to Yemen with his family; I believe it was at the time of the Gulf crisis. The narration says about the Yemenis being exiled; "…until
is taken by a man from them and he will exile the Yemeni to Yemen
He will be sent between the ages of thirty to forty years. Ali ibn Abi Talib (r.a) says; "
He will be sent between the ages of thirty and forty". It is narrated that he will appear at the age of thirty or thirty-two, after a period of absentia; "The
(Mahdi) is of my children…he will be known, then he will go missing for a period of time. Then he will appear as a youth granted divine ability at the age of thirty-two years
o that some people will desert him. He will fill the earth with justice and
equality as it was filled with injustice and oppression". Another narration states thirty years of age.
He will in a dubious manner. Talks about him will become widespread. His affair will become clear. Ali (r.a) says, "
will appear in a dubious manner to be cleared up, so talks of him will rise and his affair will become well known.
from the sky including via satellites.
A caller will proclaim his name and his father's name from the sky (via
belong to the family of Muhammad.

People's hearts will become filled with love for him and all their talks will be about him and him alone. The call from the sky will spread across the entire earth and call to everyone in their relevant language. The calls are multiple. One of them will be the call of Jibreel. Ali ibn Abi Talib says (r.a) says; "When a caller proclaims from the sky that the rights belong to the family of Muhammad, at that time the Mahdi will appear".
A loud sound from the sky (Hadda as mentioned in hadith) will be heard in his time. Some people will become scared. Others will faint and collapse. Perhaps the sound was the September 11
event, or maybe a nuclear strike, or it might be some abnormal event which will occur as a sign from Allah. The Prophet
"When the scream occurs in Ramadan, the brawl will be in Shawwaal, the tribes will separate in Dhul Qa'da,
blood will be shed in Dhul Hijja, and Muharram…What is Muharram? (

repeated this thrice) Woe! Woe! People will be killed in mass confusion". We asked, "What is the scream, oh Messenger of Allah?" He replied; "A loud noise from the sky in the second half of Ramadan on Jum
a night. It will be a loud noise which will awaken the ones asleep, make the ones who are standing to sit
bring the young girls out of their chambers,
on a Jumua night in a year of many earthquakes. So when you perform Fajr prayer on that Jumua, go home and lock your door
wrap up yourselves, cover your ears and when you sense the scream, fall in prostration to Allah and say 'Subhaanal
Quddoos, Subhaanal Quddoos, Rabbunal Quddoos'. Whoever does that will be saved. Whoever does not, will perish".
fleeing an oppressive ruler and his family being imprisoned.

A brutal and oppressive ruler will pursue him. The Mahdi will flee and one of his close companions will be killed. The narration states; "When the innocent soul is killed, and his brother, killed in Makkah in a wasteful manner, a caller will proclaim from the skies that your leader is so and so. That would be the Mahdi who will fill the earth with rights and justice."
His entire family will be taken; the young and old. Every one of them will be arrested and imprisoned. The narration states; "The Sufyani and his
will appear and nothing will be of concern to them except the family of Muhammad

…and he will send a contingency to Madina, he will kill a man there and the Mahdi and Mansoor will flee. The family of Muhammad will be taken, the young and the old. Every one of them will be arrested. The army will leave in search of the two men. The Mahdi will leave on the style of Musa; afraid and ever vigil."
That will be around the same time of discord breaking out at the death of a

The narration states; "Whoever guarantees me the death of Abdullah, I guarantee him the Qaaim (Mahdi)". This 'Abdullah' refers to a king named Abdullah. It also seems that this refers to an incident which will happen in the land of Hijaz (Makkah and Madinah). After his death, fight will break out over a treasure (oil), or at the house (Ka'ba), between three individuals coming from one household. Kingship will not settle to any of them. The Prophet
said; "Three people will fight at your treasure [at your house]. All of them are sons of a
aliph. It [kingship] will not settle to any of them. Then the black flags will come from the East. They will fight them like no other. Then the Caliph of Allah, the Mahdi, will come. So
when you hear of him, come to him and pledge to him, for he is the vicegerent of Allah, the Mahdi."
to the scholars for the cause of pledging to the
(one of the four corners of the Ka’ba)

Seven scholars will be in his pursuit. Three hundred and thirteen men would have pledged to them in advance, for the cause of pledging allegiance to the Mahdi between the Rukn and Maqaam. Abdullah ibn Masood (r.a) said; "When the trade routes become cut off, and the pathways, and when tests and trials become a lot, seven scholars from different horizons will emerge without any prior appointment. A little of three hundred men would have pledged to each of them".
The tribes will split up, form into groups, and fight will ensue in Makkah. They will hasten to their best man. The

scholars will search for him and find him in Makkah. They will bring him out from one of the houses there and force him to take the pledge between the Rukn and Maqaam. He will be forty years of age. The Prophet
said, "The Mahdi is of my children, forty years old". In another narration; "The Mahdi will emerge at forty years of age". The one who will tell of his whereabouts despite him

will be a close ally of his - a companion. The narration states; "until just before his emergence, his companion who is normally with him will come to meet others of his companions and he will ask, 'How many of you are here?' They will reply, 'Around forty men'. Then he will say, 'What will you do if you see
will say, 'By Allah! If he climbs the mountains, we will climb with him!' The Prophet
said, "The number of the companion of the battle of Badr will pledge
to him; three hundred and thirteen men and women. Those who went
will not surpass them, and those who come after will not meet them up". "So
will bring together a community for him as the

together. Allah will bring their hearts together. They will not feel sad with anyone not being with them, nor would they be happy with anyone joining them. (They will be at) the number of the companions of Badr. Those went first will not surpass them and those coming after will not meet them up.

(They will be) the number of the companions of Talut who crossed the river with him". They will be from Makkah, Yemen and the Levant. Ibn Abbaas narrates; "Allah will send the Mahdi after a state of hopelessness…they will travel to him from the Levant and bring him out from the belly of Makkah, at a house close to Safa. They will force him to take the pledge. He will then pray two units of prayer with them, the
prayer at the Maqam. Then he will climb the pulpit".
to a distance of around two miles (
pledge will be given to him after the incident of

cover the stones of Zait. Harra from there would not be far. Only about a whips length away. He will leave
to the distance of two Bareed (about two miles). Then a pledge will be given to him."
There will occur a pledge to him at Bait ul Maqdis (
) called 'the pledge of guidance'. The Prophet
said, "In Bait ul Maqdis, there will be the pledge of guidance".
An army will be sent towards him from the direction of Syria. Allah will cause that army to sink at the Baidaa. The Baidaa refers to the desert. The Prophet
said, "A person will take refuge in the house and a contingency will be sent towards them. When they reach at a Baidaa land, they will sink". The narrations indicate that it refers to the Baidaa of M
is a place located after Dhul Hulaifa which is well known as 'the wells of Ali'. Look at the narration of Abdul Aziz ibn Rufai'; he said, "
I met Abu Ja'far and said, 'She (referring to Umm Salama) only said it was Baidaa (a desert) land'. So Abu Ja'far said, 'Not at all! It is indeed the Baidaa of Madinah!' At that point, the

will become certain that he is indeed the awaited Mahdi. So the Abdaal (pious special ones) of Syria, the A'saaib (strong ones) of Iraq and the Najaaib (wise ones) of Egypt will come to him. Ka'b says; "Among them will be the noble ones of Egypt, the special pious ones of Syria and the best of the Iraqis". Thus, the
He will rule the Arabs and the
said, "The hour will not come until a man from my family lineage rules. He will liberate Constantinople and the Dailam mountains".
The first flag he will raise
The first flag the Mahdi will tie will be towards the Turks". The original 'Turks' refer to Khuz and Kirman which is nowadays Russia. Many of them migrated to

He left the lands of the two sacred mosques following the events of the Gulf crisis in 1990 C.E and was appointed as a Khateeb and Imam in a Masjid in Hudaida.

He studied in Makkah Mukarramah until the secondary level and memorized the Quran at a young age. He studied the principles of Islamic sciences in the courtyards of the Holy Mosque of Makkah and was later appointed as an Imam in one of the mosques of Makkah.
